java:S2259.name: Null Pointer Dereference
java:S2259.description: A reference that may be null is dereferenced without a null check, which can cause a NullPointerException at runtime.
java:S2259.fix_strategy: Add a null check (Objects.requireNonNull, Optional, or explicit if-null guard) before the dereference. Where possible return Optional<T> from the method instead of null.
java:S2259.example_before: String s = getValue(); int len = s.length(); // s may be null
java:S2259.example_after: String s = getValue(); if (s == null) { return; } int len = s.length();

java:S2095.name: Resource Leak
java:S2095.short: Closeable resource not closed in finally / try-with-resources
java:S2095.description: A Closeable resource (stream, connection, reader, etc.) is opened but may not be closed if an exception is thrown, causing a resource leak.
java:S2095.fix_strategy: Wrap the resource in a try-with-resources block: try (InputStream in = open()) { ... }. If Java < 7 compatibility is required, use a finally block.
java:S2095.example_before: InputStream in = new FileInputStream(file); byte[] data = in.readAllBytes();
java:S2095.example_after: try (InputStream in = new FileInputStream(file)) { byte[] data = in.readAllBytes(); }

java:S106.name: Standard Outputs Used Directly
java:S106.short: System.out / System.err used instead of a logger
java:S106.description: System.out.println and System.err.println are used for logging. These cannot be enabled/disabled at runtime and do not include structured metadata.
java:S106.fix_strategy: Replace with a SLF4J logger: private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(Foo.class); then log.info(...), log.error(...).

java:S5547.name: Weak Cipher Algorithm
java:S5547.short: Use of broken or risky cryptographic algorithm
java:S5547.description: A weak or broken cipher (DES, 3DES, RC2, RC4, Blowfish, etc.) is used. These algorithms are considered cryptographically broken.
java:S5547.fix_strategy: Replace with AES-256-GCM: Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding"). Use SecretKeySpec with a 256-bit key. Prefer Java's javax.crypto.spec.GCMParameterSpec.

java:S2068.name: Hardcoded Credentials
java:S2068.short: Password, secret, or API key hardcoded in source
java:S2068.description: A password, secret, or credential is hardcoded as a string literal. This exposes secrets in version control and compiled bytecode.
java:S2068.fix_strategy: Move credentials to environment variables or a secrets manager. Read at runtime: System.getenv("DB_PASSWORD") or inject via @Value in Spring. Never commit secrets.

java:S1192.name: Duplicated String Literal
java:S1192.short: String literal duplicated 3+ times — extract as constant
java:S1192.description: The same string literal appears 3 or more times. Duplicating strings makes refactoring error-prone and inflates the constant pool.
java:S1192.fix_strategy: Extract the string into a private static final String constant at the top of the class and reference the constant in all usages.
java:S1192.example_after: private static final String START = "START"; ... log.info(START);

java:S3776.name: Cognitive Complexity
java:S3776.description: The method has a cognitive complexity score above the threshold (default 15). High complexity makes code hard to understand and test.
java:S3776.fix_strategy: Extract sub-responsibilities into private helper methods. Replace deeply nested conditionals with early returns (guard clauses). Consider the Strategy or Command pattern for branching logic.
java:S3776.example_before: // long method with nested if/for/switch blocks
java:S3776.example_after: // split into smaller methods, each with a single responsibility

java:S1481.name: Unused Local Variable
java:S1481.short: Local variable declared but never used
java:S1481.description: A local variable is declared and assigned but never read. This wastes memory and suggests a logic error (the result of a computation is silently discarded).
java:S1481.fix_strategy: Remove the variable declaration. If the assignment has side effects (e.g. a method call), keep the call but drop the assignment.

java:S2293.name: Diamond Operator
java:S2293.short: Redundant type parameter — use diamond operator <>
java:S2293.description: Explicit type parameters are provided on the right-hand side of a generic constructor call when the compiler can infer them, making the code unnecessarily verbose.
java:S2293.fix_strategy: Replace the explicit type arguments with the diamond operator <>.

java:S2166.name: Assert Statement Side Effects
java:S2166.short: assert keyword used for runtime checking (disabled in production)
java:S2166.description: assert statements are disabled by default in production JVMs (java -ea is required). Using assert for business-logic validation creates a silent no-op in production.
java:S2166.fix_strategy: Replace assert with explicit validation: throw new IllegalArgumentException("message") or use Objects.requireNonNull / Guava Preconditions.
java:S2166.example_before: assert value != null : "value must not be null";

java:S1118.name: Utility Class Constructor
java:S1118.short: Utility classes should not have public constructors
java:S1118.description: A class with only static members should not be instantiated. A public or default constructor allows accidental instantiation.
java:S1118.fix_strategy: Add a private no-argument constructor with a comment or throw an AssertionError to prevent instantiation.
java:S1118.example_before: public class Utils { public static String format(String s) { ... } }
java:S1118.example_after: public class Utils { private Utils() { throw new AssertionError("Utility class"); } public static String format(String s) { ... } }

java:S1135.name: TODO Comment
java:S1135.short: TODO tag not tracked in issue tracker
java:S1135.description: A TODO or FIXME comment was left in the code. These should be tracked in an issue tracker, not as inline comments.
java:S1135.fix_strategy: Either resolve the TODO inline or create a ticket and replace the comment with a reference (e.g. // TODO: PROJ-123 — description).
java:S1135.example_after: // TODO: PROJ-456 — replace with proper validation (tracked)

java:S1172.name: Unused Method Parameter
java:S1172.short: Method parameter never used in method body
java:S1172.description: A method parameter is declared but never referenced inside the method body. This suggests a missing implementation or a stale signature.
java:S1172.fix_strategy: Either use the parameter in the method body or remove it (if the method is not part of a public API / override). For interface implementations, consider annotating with @SuppressWarnings("unused").
java:S1172.example_before: public void process(String input, int unused) { doWork(input); }

java:S1854.name: Dead Store
java:S1854.short: Value assigned to a variable is never read before reassignment
java:S1854.description: A value is assigned to a variable but that variable is overwritten before it is ever read, making the initial assignment dead code.
java:S1854.fix_strategy: Remove the dead assignment. If the right-hand side has side effects (method call), keep the call but discard the result.
java:S1854.example_after: int x = 0; use(x); // remove dead expensiveOp() assignment

java:S2184.name: Integer Overflow in Arithmetic
java:S2184.short: Integer arithmetic may overflow before widening cast
java:S2184.description: An integer arithmetic expression can overflow silently before it is cast to a wider type (long). This produces incorrect results for large inputs.
java:S2184.fix_strategy: Cast at least one operand to long before the operation: (long) a * b instead of (long)(a * b).
java:S2184.example_before: long total = count * price; // may overflow if count*price > Integer.MAX_VALUE

java:S2245.name: Predictable Random Seed
java:S2245.short: java.util.Random seeded with a predictable value
java:S2245.description: java.util.Random is seeded with a constant or predictable value (e.g. System.currentTimeMillis() used incorrectly), making its output guessable.
java:S2245.fix_strategy: For security-sensitive usage, replace with java.security.SecureRandom. For non-security code, use the no-arg constructor new Random() which uses a non-predictable seed.
java:S2245.example_before: Random rng = new Random(12345L); // constant seed
java:S2245.example_after: SecureRandom rng = new SecureRandom(); // cryptographically strong

java:S2647.name: Basic Authentication Over HTTP
java:S2647.short: HTTP Basic authentication used without HTTPS
java:S2647.description: HTTP Basic authentication sends credentials in Base64 encoding without encryption. Over plain HTTP this exposes credentials in transit.
java:S2647.fix_strategy: Ensure the connection uses HTTPS (TLS). Replace http:// URLs with https:// and verify the server certificate.
java:S2647.example_before: URL url = new URL("http://api.example.com/data"); // basic auth header attached

java:S2076.name: OS Command Injection
java:S2076.short: Unsanitised user input passed to Runtime.exec() or ProcessBuilder
java:S2076.description: Unsanitised user-supplied data is passed to a shell command, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary commands on the host.
java:S2076.fix_strategy: Never concatenate user input into shell commands. Use ProcessBuilder with a list of arguments (no shell interpolation). Whitelist and validate all user-supplied values.

java:S2083.name: Path Traversal
java:S2083.short: File path built from user input without normalisation
java:S2083.description: A file path is constructed using user-supplied data without sanitising '../' sequences, allowing an attacker to traverse the filesystem.
java:S2083.fix_strategy: Normalise the path with Path.normalize() and verify it starts with the expected base directory using Path.startsWith(baseDir).
java:S2083.example_before: File file = new File(baseDir, userInput); file.read();
java:S2083.example_after: Path resolved = Path.of(baseDir).resolve(userInput).normalize(); if (!resolved.startsWith(baseDir)) throw new SecurityException(); resolved.toFile().read();

java:S2629.name: Logging Arguments Evaluated Eagerly
java:S2629.short: String concatenation used in logger call — evaluates even when log level disabled
java:S2629.description: String concatenation (+ operator) inside a log call evaluates the expression even when the log level is disabled, wasting CPU cycles.
java:S2629.fix_strategy: Use parameterised logging: log.debug("Value: {}", value) instead of log.debug("Value: " + value). Check isDebugEnabled() only for complex expressions.

java:S3457.name: Printf-style Format String Mismatch
java:S3457.short: Wrong number or type of arguments to String.format()
java:S3457.description: A printf-style format string has a mismatch between the number of format specifiers and the number of arguments, causing MissingFormatArgumentException at runtime.
java:S3457.fix_strategy: Count %s/%d/etc. placeholders in the format string and ensure the argument count matches exactly. Use IDE format string inspections.
java:S3457.example_before: String msg = String.format("User %s logged in at %s", username); // missing 2nd arg
java:S3457.example_after: String msg = String.format("User %s logged in at %s", username, timestamp);

java:S1764.name: Identical Sub-expressions on Both Sides of Operator
java:S1764.short: Both sides of a binary operator are the same expression
java:S1764.description: Both operands of a binary operator are identical, which is almost always a bug (e.g. a & a, a == a, a - a).
java:S1764.fix_strategy: Review the expression and replace one of the identical operands with the correct variable or value.
java:S1764.example_before: if (a == a) { // always true — should compare to b

java:S4834.name: Thread.sleep in a Loop
java:S4834.short: Thread.sleep called inside a wait/poll loop
java:S4834.description: Calling Thread.sleep() in a busy-wait loop wastes CPU and is fragile. Prefer proper synchronisation primitives.
java:S4834.fix_strategy: Replace busy-wait with Object.wait/notify, java.util.concurrent primitives (CountDownLatch, Semaphore, CompletableFuture), or a scheduled executor.

java:S2589.name: Boolean Expression Always True or False
java:S2589.short: Condition is trivially true or false
java:S2589.description: A boolean expression or condition always evaluates to the same value (true or false) due to previous checks or logical tautologies.
java:S2589.fix_strategy: Remove the redundant condition. If the condition guards a block, remove the guard entirely (if always true) or the entire block (if always false).
java:S2589.example_before: if (x != null && x != null) { // second check is redundant

java:S3010.name: Static Field Updated in Instance Method
java:S3010.short: Instance method writes to a static field — thread-safety issue
java:S3010.description: An instance method updates a static (class-level) field without synchronisation, causing a race condition in multi-threaded environments.
java:S3010.fix_strategy: Make the field an instance field, or synchronise access with synchronized or an AtomicXxx type (AtomicInteger, AtomicReference, etc.).
java:S3010.example_before: static int counter; void increment() { counter++; }
java:S3010.example_after: static final AtomicInteger counter = new AtomicInteger(); void increment() { counter.incrementAndGet(); }

java:S2274.name: Object.wait Called Without Loop
java:S2274.short: Object.wait() not inside a while loop — susceptible to spurious wakeups
java:S2274.description: Object.wait() should always be called inside a while loop that re-checks the condition. A spurious wakeup can cause incorrect behaviour if an if statement is used instead.
java:S2274.fix_strategy: Replace if (condition) { obj.wait(); } with while (condition) { obj.wait(); }.

java:S1206.name: equals Without hashCode
java:S1206.short: Class overrides equals() but not hashCode()
java:S1206.description: A class that overrides equals() must also override hashCode() to maintain the contract: equal objects must have equal hash codes. Violation causes bugs in HashMap, HashSet, etc.
java:S1206.fix_strategy: Override hashCode() using the same fields as equals(). Use Objects.hash(field1, field2) for a concise implementation.
java:S1206.example_before: @Override public boolean equals(Object o) { ... } // no hashCode
java:S1206.example_after: @Override public boolean equals(Object o) { ... } @Override public int hashCode() { return Objects.hash(id, name); }

java:S2387.name: Child Class Field Hides Parent Field
java:S2387.short: Field in subclass shadows field with same name in superclass
java:S2387.description: A field declared in a child class has the same name as a field in the parent class, creating a shadowing that is confusing and error-prone.
java:S2387.fix_strategy: Rename the field in the child class to avoid shadowing. If the same field is intended, remove the child declaration and use the parent's field directly (make it protected if needed).
java:S2387.example_before: class Parent { int count; } class Child extends Parent { int count; // shadows }
java:S2387.example_after: class Parent { protected int count; } class Child extends Parent { /* uses parent count */ }

java:S2189.name: Infinite Loop
java:S2189.short: Loop condition is never false — infinite loop
java:S2189.description: A loop's termination condition is never reached, creating an infinite loop that will hang the thread or process.
java:S2189.fix_strategy: Ensure the loop variable is modified inside the loop body, or add a break/return statement for the exit condition.
java:S2189.example_before: for (int i = 0; i < 10; ) { process(i); } // i never incremented

java:S1123.name: Deprecated Code Not Documented
java:S1123.description: A method is annotated with @Deprecated but lacks the @deprecated Javadoc tag explaining why it is deprecated and what the replacement is.
java:S1123.fix_strategy: Add a Javadoc comment with @deprecated explaining the reason and pointing to the replacement: /** @deprecated Use {@link #newMethod()} instead. */
java:S1123.example_after: /** @deprecated Use {@link #newMethod()} instead — will be removed in v3.0. */ @Deprecated public void oldMethod() { ... }

java:S3252.name: Static Member Accessed via Instance Reference
java:S3252.short: Static field or method accessed through an instance variable
java:S3252.description: A static member (field or method) is accessed via an instance reference. This is misleading and can cause bugs if the variable is null or polymorphic dispatch is expected.
java:S3252.fix_strategy: Always access static members through the class name: ClassName.staticMethod() or ClassName.STATIC_FIELD.
